reason: tag1consulting/ai-pr-review referencing itself (@main, a tag, or a SHA) is not a supply-chain risk — it IS the action being run. Flagging the tool's own canonical invocation as an unpinned third-party dependency is a false positive.

uses: tag1consulting/ai-pr-review@main in ai-review.yml is intentional. Consumers deliberately pin to @main (or a tag/SHA) for this specific action. Flagging it as a supply-chain risk is a false positive — the action is the reviewed tool itself or is a known trusted dependency.
Shellcheck misparses the heredoc inside the if condition in run-shellcheck.sh and emits SC1072+SC1073 as a pair; bash -n passes cleanly. Suppressing all shellcheck findings on this file.
Intentional: || echo '0' in arithmetic contexts provides a safe default when grep finds no match. Defaulting to 0 lines does not suppress any review output — it is the conservative path.
Intentional: merge_findings skipping a corrupt batch with WARNING is by design. Aborting the entire review on one bad batch is worse than losing one agent's findings.
Intentional: || echo '[]' on shellcheck failure is graceful degradation. Shellcheck is a nice-to-have lint pass; its failure should not block the AI review.

MARKER_PREFIX is a hardcoded constant (<!-- ai-pr-review-summary) containing no jq metacharacters. gh api does not support --arg, so shell interpolation into --jq is the only option. The interpolation is safe for this controlled value.
review_id always originates from gh api JSON output as an integer. Shell-interpolating an integer into a jq filter is safe; no string quoting or metacharacter risk applies to integer values.

Intentional: get_last_reviewed_sha emits a WARNING on API failure and returns empty, triggering a full PR diff. This is the safe fallback — a full re-review is correct behavior when the last SHA is unknown, and the WARNING distinguishes it from a genuine first run.

LLMs flag GitHub Action versions released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent' or claim an older major version is 'latest'. Scoped to findings that reference an actions/ path. The verify field causes review.sh to confirm the tag/release exists via GitHub API before accepting the suppression — if the version genuinely does not exist (404), the finding is kept.

match.pattern: (does not exist( as (a |an )?(released |valid |published )?(version|release|tag))?|is not a (released|valid|published) version|no such (release|version|tag) exists|(latest|current) (published |stable |major )*(version|release) is v[0-9]+|unreleased version|references? a non-existent (tag|version|release)|may not be a (published|valid|released) (release|version|tag)|might not (exist|be published|be a valid)|not (yet )?published|could not (be verified|exist)).{0,300}actions/|actions/.{0,300}(does not exist( as (a |an )?(released |valid |published )?(version|release|tag))?|is not a (released|valid|published) version|no such (release|version|tag) exists|(latest|current) (published |stable |major )*(version|release) is v[0-9]+|unreleased version|references? a non-existent (tag|version|release)|may not be a (published|valid|released) (release|version|tag)|might not (exist|be published|be a valid)|not (yet )?published|could not (be verified|exist))

reason: LLMs flag npm package versions released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent'. Scoped to findings with pkg@version or "pkg": "version" patterns. The verify field causes review.sh to confirm existence on registry.npmjs.org before suppressing.

match.pattern: (does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version).{0,200}[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+@[0-9]|[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+@[0-9].{0,200}(does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version)

reason: LLMs flag PyPI package versions released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent'. Scoped to findings with pkg==version patterns. The verify field causes review.sh to confirm existence on pypi.org before suppressing.

match.pattern: (does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version).{0,200}[a-zA-Z0-9._-]==[0-9]|[a-zA-Z0-9._-]==[0-9].{0,200}(does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version)

reason: LLMs flag Go module versions released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent'. Scoped to findings with module@vX.Y.Z patterns (requires a domain-like module path). The verify field causes review.sh to confirm existence on proxy.golang.org before suppressing.

match.pattern: (does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version).{0,200}[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}/[a-zA-Z0-9._/@-]+@v[0-9]|[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+\.[a-zA-Z]{2,}/[a-zA-Z0-9._/@-]+@v[0-9].{0,200}(does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version)

reason: LLMs flag Rust crate versions released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent'. Scoped to findings with pkg = "version" or pkg@version Cargo patterns. The verify field causes review.sh to confirm existence on crates.io before suppressing.

match.pattern: (does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version).{0,200}[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ = "[0-9]|[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+ = "[0-9].{0,200}(does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such version)

reason: actions/upload-artifact and actions/download-artifact have intentionally offset major version numbers — they are separate packages released independently. upload-artifact@v7 is fully compatible with download-artifact@v8; the download-artifact@v8 README explicitly uses upload-artifact@v7 in its examples. Flagging mismatched major versions as an incompatibility is a false positive for this specific action pair.

semgrep's bash.lang.security.ifs-tampering rule flags any IFS= assignment, but all IFS= uses in this repo are either (a) loop-local 'while IFS= read -r ...' — the standard bash idiom for preserving whitespace in read — or (b) subshell-scoped inside $(IFS=X; echo "${arr[*]}") — the standard idiom for joining a bash array with a delimiter. Neither tampers with the global IFS. The rule does not recognize subshell/loop scoping, so this is a false positive.

match.pattern: ifs-tampering|IFS affects how splitting takes place

reason: LLMs flag Docker Hub image tags released after their training cutoff as 'nonexistent'. Scoped to findings with image:tag patterns. The verify field causes review.sh to confirm existence on hub.docker.com before suppressing. Private registries (GHCR, GCR, ECR) are excluded as they require auth.

match.pattern: (does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such (tag|version|image)).{0,200}([a-zA-Z0-9._-]+/)?[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+:[0-9]|([a-zA-Z0-9._-]+/)?[a-zA-Z0-9._-]+:[0-9].{0,200}(does not exist|unreleased|not (a |an )?(valid |published |released )?version|no such (tag|version|image))

reason: Unverified secret detections in test/fixture/example files are almost always intentional mock data (fake tokens, placeholder credentials). The run-trufflehog.sh script already demotes these to Low/confidence-40 and tags them '[test file — likely mock data]', but this rule provides explicit suppression as a safety net. Verified secrets in test files are NOT suppressed — a real leaked credential is critical regardless of location.
